note: Canonical golden DatHost cs2_settings for the DGLS match server (id 6824c4d10bf4444f5817d188). Provisioning PUTs these (dotted, e.g. `cs2_settings.game_mode`) to /game-servers/{id} before start, to overwrite any recreational-mode drift made in the panel between matches. `game_mode: competitive` is intentional — a 'competitive-style wingman' hybrid (for xplay consistency), with the wingman-specific rules (round count, warmup, overtime, economy) layered on top via cfg/gamemode_competitive2v2_server.cfg and cfg/MatchZy/live_override.cfg. Secrets (rcon, passwords, FTP/MySQL, GSLT) are intentionally omitted — never commit them. `enable_gotv` must stay `true`: MatchZy's demo recording is a `tv_record` wrapper that produces no demo without an active GOTV host (see docs/cs2-stack-reference.md), and `cfg/server.cfg` sets `tv_autorecord 0` so MatchZy — not GOTV auto-record — controls recording. `disable_workshop_command_filtering` is `true` because CS2's workshop command filter (active whenever a workshop map is loaded — always, for this league) otherwise discards cvars set via any cfg `exec` or plugin `Server.ExecuteCommand`, so cfg-file cvars (live_override.cfg's economy/round settings and its `sv_spec_hear` spectator-chat cvar) would silently fail to apply.

per_match_overrides.note: Map selection is set at provision time, per match — NOT part of the static golden baseline (src/lib/dathost.ts excludes these keys from the static PUT and sets them separately in applyGoldenSettings). `workshop_collection` mode does not behave reliably on this server — confirmed live — so every provision requires a resolved single map; applyGoldenSettings throws rather than falling back to it.
per_match_overrides.cs2_settings.workshop_single_map_id: <the picked map's Steam workshop id, extracted from maps.workshop_url>
